title: Aweary.
content:
The clouds that vex the upper deep
    Stay not the white sail of the moon;
    And lips may moan, and hearts may weep,
    The sad old earth goes rolling on.

    O'er smiling vale, and sighing lake,
    One shadow cold is overthrown;
    And souls may faint, and hearts may break,
    The sad old earth goes rolling on.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Marietta Holley, titled "Aweary.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
